# register_inflight registers a cell as in-flight in the state file.
# Reads current entries, adds this cell, removes dead PIDs, writes back.
register_inflight() {
  local cell_id="$1" cell_dir="$2"
  local state_dir
  state_dir="$(in_flight_state_dir)"
  mkdir -p "${state_dir}"
  local json_path
  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"

  local current_json="{\"cells\":[]}"
  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] && current_json="$(cat "${json_path}")" || true

  # Prune dead PIDs by filtering in bash
  local live_cells=()
  local cell_json pid
  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && kill -0 "${pid}" 2>/dev/null; then
      live_cells+=("${cell_json}")
    fi
  done < <(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' <<<"${current_json}")

  # Rebuild with live cells
  current_json="$(printf '%s\n' "${live_cells[@]}" | jq -s '{cells:.}')"

  # Add this cell
  current_json="$(jq \
    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
    --arg cell_dir "${cell_dir}" \
    --argjson pid $$ \
    '.cells += [{cell_id:$cell_id, cell_dir:$cell_dir, pid:$pid}]' \
    <<<"${current_json}")"

  printf '%s\n' "${current_json}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
}


# unregister_inflight removes a cell from the in-flight state file.
unregister_inflight() {
  local cell_id="$1"
  local json_path
  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0

  local updated
  updated="$(jq \
    --arg cell_id "${cell_id}" \
    '.cells |= map(select(.cell_id != $cell_id))' \
    "${json_path}")"

  printf '%s\n' "${updated}" >"${json_path}.tmp"
  mv "${json_path}.tmp" "${json_path}"
}

# interrupt_inflight_cells is called during signal handling to terminate
# in-flight cells and stop their Harbor containers before the process pool is
# reaped.
interrupt_inflight_cells() {
  local json_path
  json_path="$(in_flight_json_path)"
  [[ -f "${json_path}" ]] || return 0

  local cells
  cells="$(jq -r '.cells[] | @json' "${json_path}")" || return 0

  local cell_id cell_dir pid
  while IFS= read -r cell_json; do
    [[ -z "${cell_json}" ]] && continue
    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    cell_dir="$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"${cell_json}")"
    pid="$(jq -r '.pid' <<<"${cell_json}")"

    # Try to kill the cell's process group
    if [[ -n "${pid}" ]] && (( pid > 0 )); then
      kill -TERM -"${pid}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi

    # Stop the deterministic Harbor container name first, then fall back to a
    # label search in case the executor is older or the container was renamed.
    local container_name
    container_name="$(cell_container_name "${cell_id}")"
    docker stop "${container_name}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    docker ps -q --filter "label=fizeau-cell=${cell_id}" 2>/dev/null \
      | xargs -r docker stop >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  done <<<"${cells}"
}

preflight_failures=0

preflight_ok() {
  printf 'preflight: [ok] %s\n' "$1"
}

preflight_fail() {
  printf 'preflight: [fail] %s\n' "$1"

rm -f "${attempt_of}/cell-state.json"
  fi

  # Unregister from in-flight (trap will also call this on return)
  unregister_inflight "${cell_id}"
  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
}

# budget_or_run_cell either writes a budget-halted placeholder (when the
# budget cap has been hit) or invokes run_one_cell. On success it appends the
# cell's cost into <out>/budget.json so the halt flag flips for the next
# cell. cap="" disables budget machinery entirely. interrupted_flag="" disables
# interrupt checking.
budget_or_run_cell() {
  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" adapter="${10}" attempt_of="${11}"
  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}" cap="${14}" interrupted_flag="${15:-}"

  local cell_dir
  if [[ -n "${cap}" ]] && budget_is_halted "${out}"; then
    cell_dir="$(write_budget_halted_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
                  "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
                  "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${cap}" "${te_ver}" "${hd}")"
    printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
    return 0
  fi

  cell_dir="$(run_one_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
                "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
                "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${adapter}" "${attempt_of}" \
                "${te_ver}" "${hd}" "${interrupted_flag}")"

  if [[ -n "${cap}" && -f "${cell_dir}/report.json" ]]; then
    local cell_id cost
    cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id // ""' "${cell_dir}/report.json")"
    cost="$(jq -r '.cost_usd_at_run_time // 0' "${cell_dir}/report.json")"
    [[ -n "${cost}" ]] || cost=0
    append_budget_cell "${out}" "${cell_id}" "${task}" "${profile_id}" "${cost}" >/dev/null
  fi
  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
}

# run_cell_worker executes one cell in a dedicated setsid-backed subprocess so
# each cell gets its own process group and concurrency-group lock lifetime.
run_cell_worker() {
  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" adapter="${10}" attempt_of="${11}"
  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}" cap="${14}" interrupted_flag="${15:-}"
  budget_or_run_cell "${out}" "${profile_id}" "${profile_file}" \
    "${framework}" "${dataset}" "${dataset_dir}" "${task}" \
    "${rep_idx}" "${total_reps}" "${adapter}" "${attempt_of}" \
    "${te_ver}" "${hd}" "${cap}" "${interrupted_flag}"

       started_at:$now,
       finished_at:$now,
       rep:$rep,
       profile:$profile,
       command:[],
       env_redacted:{},
       artifacts:{fiz_txt:"fiz.txt", fiz_err:"fiz.err", session_dir:"session"},
       result:{},
       final_status:"budget_halted",
       invalid_class:"",
       process_outcome:"setup_failed",
       note:$note,
       cost_usd_at_run_time:$cost,
       task_executor_version:$te_ver,
       harbor_runner_image_digest:$hd
     }' >"${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp"
  mv "${cell_dir}/report.json.tmp" "${cell_dir}/report.json"
  printf '%s\n' "${cell_dir}"
}

# Run one cell start-to-finish: cell-state sentinel, harness-adapter command,
# task-spec composition, task-executor invocation with retry, report.json
# composition, sentinel cleanup, optional back-write of superseded_by into
# the prior (attempt_of) cell. Registers as in-flight at start, unregisters at end.
run_one_cell() {
  local out="$1" profile_id="$2" profile_file="$3"
  local framework="$4" dataset="$5" dataset_dir="$6" task="$7"
  local rep_idx="$8" total_reps="$9" harness_adapter="${10}"
  local attempt_of="${11}"
  local te_ver="${12}" hd="${13}"
  local interrupted_flag="${14:-}"

  local cell_id cell_dir
  cell_id="$(new_cell_id)"

# Captures attempts log to ${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt for forensics.
run_executor_with_retry() {
  local task_spec="$1" cell_dir="$2" executor="$3"
  local stderr_file
  stderr_file="$(mktemp)"
  local max_attempts="${BENCH_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS}"
  local backoff="${BENCH_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE}"
  local attempt=1
  local status=0
  local retry_log="${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt"
  : >"${retry_log}"
  while :; do
    : >"${stderr_file}"
    set +e
    printf '%s' "${task_spec}" | "${executor}" 2>"${stderr_file}" >/dev/null
    status=$?
    set -e
    printf 'attempt=%d status=%d\n' "${attempt}" "${status}" >>"${retry_log}"
    if [[ ${status} -eq 0 ]]; then
      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
      return 0
    fi
    cat "${stderr_file}" >>"${retry_log}"
    if (( attempt > max_attempts )) || ! is_transient_stderr "${stderr_file}"; then
      cat "${stderr_file}" >&2
      rm -f "${stderr_file}"
      return "${status}"
    fi
    warn "cell $(basename "${cell_dir}"): transient error (attempt ${attempt}); sleeping ${backoff}s"
    sleep "${backoff}"
    backoff=$((backoff * 2))
    attempt=$((attempt + 1))
  done
}

# resolve_tasks_dir derives the host tasks dir for terminal-bench cells.
# Honors BENCH_TASKS_DIR override (used by tests).
resolve_tasks_dir() {
  local framework="$1"
  if [[ -n "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR:-}" ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR}"
    return 0
  fi
  case "${framework}" in
    terminal-bench) printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT}" ;;
    *)              printf '%s\n' "${BENCH_TASKS_DIR_DEFAULT}" ;;
  esac
}

# Count existing terminal cells matching (profile_id, task) under <out>; also
# emit lists of invalid cells and orphan cells (cells with cell-state.json
# and no terminal report.json). Lines are written to the named output paths.
inspect_existing_cells() {
  local out="$1" dataset_dir="$2" task="$3" profile_id="$4"
  local terminal_out="$5" invalid_out="$6" orphan_out="$7"
  : >"${terminal_out}"
  : >"${invalid_out}"
  : >"${orphan_out}"
  local task_dir="${out}/cells/${dataset_dir}/${task}"
  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || return 0
  local cell
  shopt -s nullglob
  for cell in "${task_dir}"/*/; do
    local cdir="${cell%/}"
    local report="${cdir}/report.json"
    local state="${cdir}/cell-state.json"
    if [[ -f "${report}" ]]; then
      local pid status inv
      pid="$(jq -r '.profile.id // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      [[ "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
      status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      inv="$(jq -r '.invalid_class // ""' "${report}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      if [[ -n "${inv}" ]]; then
        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${invalid_out}"
        continue
      fi

fi
      if is_terminal_status "${status}"; then
        printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${terminal_out}"
      fi
      continue
    fi
    if [[ -f "${state}" ]]; then
      local pid
      pid="$(jq -r '.profile // ""' "${state}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
      [[ -z "${pid}" || "${pid}" == "${profile_id}" ]] || continue
      printf '%s\n' "${cdir}" >>"${orphan_out}"
    fi
  done
  shopt -u nullglob
}

# compute_cell_cost prints the cost_usd_at_run_time for a single cell. Uses
# profile.pricing × token streams pulled from the cell's result.json. Token
# field names follow the canonical report-level naming
# (input_tokens / output_tokens / cached_input_tokens) and are also probed
# under the harbor totals naming (total_prompt_tokens / total_completion_tokens
# / total_cached_input_tokens) so harness-native token reporters land in the
# same accumulator.
#
# When profile.pricing is all zero (or unset) and the executor wrote an
# explicit cost_usd / total_cost_usd in result.json, the explicit value is
# honored — that lets harnesses that observe API-reported cost directly
# participate in budget enforcement.
compute_cell_cost() {
  local result_json="${1:-}" profile_json="${2:-}"
  [[ -n "${result_json}" ]] || result_json='{}'
  [[ -n "${profile_json}" ]] || profile_json='{}'
  jq -nr \
    --argjson result "${result_json}" \
    --argjson profile "${profile_json}" \
    '
      def num(v): if (v == null) then 0 else (v | tonumber? // 0) end;
      ($profile.pricing // {}) as $p
      | num($p.input_usd_per_mtok)         as $pi
      | num($p.output_usd_per_mtok)        as $po
      | num($p.cached_input_usd_per_mtok)  as $pc
      | num($result.input_tokens
            // $result.total_prompt_tokens
            // $result.n_input_tokens
            // 0)                          as $ti
      | num($result.output_tokens
            // $result.total_completion_tokens
            // $result.n_output_tokens
            // 0)                          as $to
      | num($result.cached_input_tokens
            // $result.total_cached_input_tokens
            // $result.n_cached_input_tokens
            // 0)                          as $tc
      | num($result.cost_usd // $result.total_cost_usd // 0) as $reported
      | (($ti * $pi) + ($to * $po) + ($tc * $pc)) / 1000000  as $priced
      | (if $priced > 0 then $priced
         elif ($pi + $po + $pc) == 0 then $reported
         else 0
         end)
      | tostring
    '
}

# budget_path prints the canonical budget.json path under <out>.
budget_path() {
  printf '%s\n' "$1/budget.json"
}

# init_budget writes <out>/budget.json with the configured cap and an empty
# accumulator. Idempotent: preserves existing cells/total when the cap
# matches (resume case).
init_budget() {
  local out="$1" cap="$2"
  local bp
  bp="$(budget_path "${out}")"
  if [[ -f "${bp}" ]]; then
    local prev_cap
    prev_cap="$(jq -r '.max_cost_usd // ""' "${bp}" 2>/dev/null || true)"
    if [[ "${prev_cap}" == "${cap}" ]]; then
      return 0
    fi
  fi
  jq -n \
    --argjson cap "${cap}" \
    '{max_cost_usd:$cap, total_cost_usd:0, halted:false, cells:[]}' \
    >"${bp}.tmp"
  mv "${bp}.tmp" "${bp}"
}

# the post-update total_cost_usd. Halted flag flips true when the running

# Write <out>/sweep.json with the executor + image identifiers. Called once

write_sweep_metadata() {
  local out="$1" te_ver="$2" hd="$3"

}

# compose_env_redacted prints command-spec env with secret_env_keys replaced
# by a redaction sentinel. Order-insensitive; entries missing from env are
# ignored.
compose_env_redacted() {
  local env_json="$1" secret_keys_json="$2"
  jq -c --argjson secrets "${secret_keys_json}" '
    . as $env
    | reduce ($secrets[]? | tostring) as $k
        ($env; if has($k) then .[$k] = "***REDACTED***" else . end)
  ' <<<"${env_json}"
}

# is_transient_stderr greps the executor's stderr for known transient error
# signatures: connection refused/reset, HTTP 5xx, unexpected/EOF parse.
is_transient_stderr() {
  local f="$1"
  [[ -s "${f}" ]] || return 1
  LC_ALL=C grep -qE 'connection refused|connection reset|HTTP 5[0-9][0-9]|status[ =:]?5[0-9][0-9]|status_code=5[0-9][0-9]|unexpected EOF|EOF parse|EOF while|temporarily unavailable' "${f}"
}

# Invoke the task-executor with bounded exponential backoff on transient
# errors. Returns 0 on success; non-zero on terminal failure after retries.
# Captures attempts log to ${cell_dir}/retry-log.txt for forensics.

print_plan() {

      bs_file="$(resolve_bench_set_file "${bs_id}")"
      framework="$(bench_set_framework "${bs_file}")"
      dataset="$(bench_set_dataset "${bs_file}")"
      reps="$(bench_set_reps "${bs_file}")"
      task_executor="$(default_task_executor_for_framework "${framework}")"
      tasks="$(resolve_tasks "${bs_file}")"
      i=0
      while IFS= read -r task; do
        [[ -n "${task}" ]] || continue
        i=$((i + 1))
        for ((rep = 1; rep <= reps; rep++)); do
          n=$((rep))
          printf 'profile=%s\tbench_set=%s\tframework=%s\tdataset=%s\ttask=%s\trep=%d/%d\ttask_executor=%s\n' \
            "${profile_id}" "${bs_id}" "${framework}" "${dataset}" \
            "${task}" "${n}" "${reps}" "${task_executor}"
        done
      done <<<"${tasks}"
      [[ "${i}" -gt 0 ]] || die "bench-set ${bs_id}: no tasks resolved"
    done
  done
}

preflight_ok() {

preflight_check_tool() {
  local tool="$1" label="${2:-$1}"

    preflight_ok "${label}: $(command -v "${tool}")"
  else
    preflight_fail "${label}: missing from PATH"
  fi
}

preflight_check_docker() {
  if ! command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    preflight_fail "docker: missing from PATH"
    return 0
  fi
  if docker info >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    preflight_ok "docker: daemon reachable"
  else
    preflight_fail "docker: daemon unreachable"
  fi
}

preflight_check_state_dir() {
  local state_dir="${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR}"
  local probe
  if ! mkdir -p "${state_dir}/locks" 2>/dev/null; then
    preflight_fail "state dir writable: ${state_dir}"
    return 0
  fi
  probe="${state_dir}/.preflight-write-test.$$"
  if : >"${probe}" 2>/dev/null; then
    rm -f "${probe}"
    preflight_ok "state dir writable: ${state_dir}"
  else
    preflight_fail "state dir writable: ${state_dir}"
  fi
}

preflight_check_harbor_image() {
  local source_sha cached_sha build_output
  source_sha="$(compute_harbor_source_sha)"
  cached_sha="$(cached_harbor_image_sha "${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}")"
  if [[ -n "${cached_sha}" && "${cached_sha}" == "${source_sha}" ]]; then
    preflight_ok "harbor-runner image up to date: ${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG} (${cached_sha})"
    return 0
  fi

  if [[ ! -x "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" ]]; then
    preflight_fail "harbor-runner image buildable: build script missing or not executable"

  if build_output="$(IMAGE_TAG="${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}" "${HARBOR_BUILD_SCRIPT}" 2>&1)"; then
    preflight_ok "harbor-runner image buildable: ${HARBOR_IMAGE_TAG}"
  else
    preflight_fail "harbor-runner image buildable: ${build_output}"
  fi
}

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Build the fizeau-harbor-runner Docker image with a deterministic
# image-content-sha label computed from harbor_adapters/, harbor_agent.py,
# and Dockerfile.harbor-runner. Re-running with no source changes reuses
# the cached image (label sha unchanged, layers from Docker build cache).
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../.." && pwd)"

IMAGE_TAG="${IMAGE_TAG:-fizeau-harbor-runner:latest}"
DOCKERFILE="${SCRIPT_DIR}/Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
ADAPTERS_DIR="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_adapters"
HARBOR_AGENT_PATH="${SCRIPT_DIR}/harbor_agent.py"

compute_content_sha() {
  {
    LC_ALL=C find "${ADAPTERS_DIR}" -type f \
      | LC_ALL=C sort \
      | while IFS= read -r f; do
          printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "$f" | awk '{print $1}')" "${f#${SCRIPT_DIR}/}"
        done
    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${HARBOR_AGENT_PATH}" | awk '{print $1}')" "harbor_agent.py"
    printf '%s  %s\n' "$(sha256sum "${DOCKERFILE}" | awk '{print $1}')" "Dockerfile.harbor-runner"
  } | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}'
}

CONTENT_SHA="$(compute_content_sha)"

docker build \
  --file "${DOCKERFILE}" \
  --label "image-content-sha=${CONTENT_SHA}" \
  --tag "${IMAGE_TAG}" \
  "${REPO_ROOT}"

printf 'image-content-sha=%s\n' "${CONTENT_SHA}"
printf 'tag=%s\n' "${IMAGE_TAG}"

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.

Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. - If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.

With the runner skeleton (A.1), shell harness-adapters (A.2), and harbor-runner image (A.3) in place, the runner still needs the execution engine: per-cell loop, resume logic, concurrency-group flocks, signal handling, and the preflight subcommand.

ROOT CAUSE
These behaviors live in cmd/bench/matrix.go's cell-execution block (process-group setsid, per-group locks, SIGINT/SIGTERM trap, resume-by-report.json). They must move to bash to complete the runner.

PROPOSED FIX
In scripts/benchmark/benchmark, implement:
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; pipe profile.json → harness-adapters/<name> → command-spec.json; compose task-spec.json; pipe → task-executors/<name> (which writes cell_dir/result.json); compose cell_dir/report.json; delete cell-state.json on terminal status.
  - Resume: skip cell when cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status. --force-rerun overrides. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR orphan cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock; setsid for each cell; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each in-flight cell process group AND `docker stop` each in-flight fizeau-harbor-<cell-id> container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
  - `preflight` subcommand: verify yq/jq/docker/setsid/flock present, fizeau-harbor-runner image buildable or pullable, required state dirs writable; exits non-zero with a checklist on failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not delete cmd/bench/matrix.go or harbor_adapters/*.py here — a later PR removes them.
- Do not change adapter contracts established in A.2.
- Preserve every parent non-scope item.

AC
1. End-to-end: `./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B --reps 1 --jobs 2 --out tmp/` runs cells via shell adapter + harbor task-executor and produces report.json per cell; covered by TestBenchmarkRunnerEndToEnd (skips when docker unavailable).
2. TestBenchmarkRunnerResumeSkipsCompleted asserts re-run with same --out skips cells with terminal report.json unless --force-rerun.
3. TestBenchmarkRunnerRetryInvalid asserts --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json.
4. TestBenchmarkRunnerConcurrencyGroupFlock asserts two cells in the same concurrency-group serialize via the documented lock path.
5. TestBenchmarkRunnerSignalHandling asserts SIGTERM stops new cells, terminates in-flight cell process groups, and issues `docker stop` for in-flight harbor containers within 30s before SIGKILL.
6. TestBenchmarkRunnerPreflight asserts `./benchmark preflight` returns 0 on healthy host and non-zero with a per-check checklist when a dependency is missing.
7. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestBenchmarkRunnerEndToEnd drives a full cell with shell adapter + harbor executor (docker-gated skip).
2. TestBenchmarkRunnerResumeSkipsCompleted verifies resume-by-report.json and --force-rerun override.

4. TestBenchmarkRunnerConcurrencyGroupFlock verifies per-group flock serialization at the documented state-dir path.
5. TestBenchmarkRunnerSignalHandling verifies SIGTERM stops accepting cells, kills cell process groups, docker-stops in-flight harbor containers, escalates to SIGKILL after 30s.
6. TestBenchmarkRunnerPreflight verifies preflight subcommand passes on healthy host and reports checklist failures.
7. cd cli && go test ./... passes.

data.text: I found the preflight hang: the test helper is recursively re-invoking the stub `bash` because it captured a non-absolute `REAL_BASH`. I’m switching the helper to resolve absolute paths for the host binaries so the stub PATH can safely shadow them.

data.text: The hang is from the preflight stubs themselves, not the benchmark. Their `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang re-enters the stub PATH and recursively calls the stub `bash`. I’m switching those generated wrappers to an absolute bash shebang so the stubs no longer self-recurse.
I’ve fixed the stub shebang recursion. I’m cleaning up the still-hung benchmark test process now so I can rerun the corrected preflight case from a clean state.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

   </instructions>   <bead id="fizeau-b49d5ea7">     <title>bench-pr-A.4: per-cell loop + flock concurrency + signal handling + preflight</title>     <description> PROBLEM
With the runner skeleton (A.1), shell harness-adapters (A.2), and harbor-runner image (A.3) in place, the runner still needs the execution engine: per-cell loop, resume logic, concurrency-group flocks, signal handling, and the preflight subcommand.

ROOT CAUSE
These behaviors live in cmd/bench/matrix.go's cell-execution block (process-group setsid, per-group locks, SIGINT/SIGTERM trap, resume-by-report.json). They must move to bash to complete the runner.

PROPOSED FIX
In scripts/benchmark/benchmark, implement:
  - Per-cell loop: write cell-state.json sentinel; pipe profile.json → harness-adapters/<name> → command-spec.json; compose task-spec.json; pipe → task-executors/<name> (which writes cell_dir/result.json); compose cell_dir/report.json; delete cell-state.json on terminal status.
  - Resume: skip cell when cell_dir/report.json exists with terminal final_status. --force-rerun overrides. --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class OR orphan cell-state.json.
  - Concurrency: flock per concurrency-group at ${FIZEAU_BENCH_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/fizeau-benchmark}/locks/<group>.lock; setsid for each cell; background-job pool capped by --jobs.
  - Signal handling: trap SIGINT/SIGTERM. Stop accepting new cells. SIGTERM each in-flight cell process group AND `docker stop` each in-flight fizeau-harbor-<cell-id> container. Wait 30s, then SIGKILL.
  - `preflight` subcommand: verify yq/jq/docker/setsid/flock present, fizeau-harbor-runner image buildable or pullable, required state dirs writable; exits non-zero with a checklist on failure.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not delete cmd/bench/matrix.go or harbor_adapters/*.py here — a later PR removes them.
- Do not change adapter contracts established in A.2.
- Preserve every parent non-scope item.

AC
1. End-to-end: `./benchmark --profile P --bench-set B --reps 1 --jobs 2 --out tmp/` runs cells via shell adapter + harbor task-executor and produces report.json per cell; covered by TestBenchmarkRunnerEndToEnd (skips when docker unavailable).
2. TestBenchmarkRunnerResumeSkipsCompleted asserts re-run with same --out skips cells with terminal report.json unless --force-rerun.
3. TestBenchmarkRunnerRetryInvalid asserts --retry-invalid reruns cells with non-empty invalid_class or orphan cell-state.json.
4. TestBenchmarkRunnerConcurrencyGroupFlock asserts two cells in the same concurrency-group serialize via the documented lock path.
5. TestBenchmarkRunnerSignalHandling asserts SIGTERM stops new cells, terminates in-flight cell process groups, and issues `docker stop` for in-flight harbor containers within 30s before SIGKILL.
6. TestBenchmarkRunnerPreflight asserts `./benchmark preflight` returns 0 on healthy host and non-zero with a per-check checklist when a dependency is missing.
7. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestBenchmarkRunnerEndToEnd drives a full cell with shell adapter + harbor executor (docker-gated skip).
2. TestBenchmarkRunnerResumeSkipsCompleted verifies resume-by-report.json and --force-rerun override.
3. TestBenchmarkRunnerRetryInvalid verifies --retry-invalid semantics.
4. TestBenchmarkRunnerConcurrencyGroupFlock verifies per-group flock serialization at the documented state-dir path.
5. TestBenchmarkRunnerSignalHandling verifies SIGTERM stops accepting cells, kills cell process groups, docker-stops in-flight harbor containers, escalates to SIGKILL after 30s.
6. TestBenchmarkRunnerPreflight verifies preflight subcommand passes on healthy host and reports checklist failures.
7. cd cli && go test ./... passes.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Integration test for the ./benchmark run path (bead fizeau-a2fd070b).
#
# Exercises all five ACs of the bead description:
#
#  1. ./benchmark --profile sindri-lucebox --bench-set tb-2-1-canary produces
#     cells with report.json embedding profile, command, env_redacted +
#     fiz.txt, fiz.err, session/ artifacts.
#  2. <out>/sweep.json captures task_executor_version +
#     harbor_runner_image_digest; each cell's report.json references them.
#  3. Re-running without --force-rerun skips cells with terminal report.json.
#  4. --retry-invalid reruns invalid cells; new cell links via attempt_of and
#     prior cell receives superseded_by back-link.
#  5. Transient 5xx triggers bounded exponential backoff + eventual success.
#
# Uses HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 (the harbor task-executor's documented
# dry-run mode) for ACs 1–4 so docker is not required. AC5 swaps in a stub
# executor (BENCH_TASK_EXECUTOR_OVERRIDE) that emits 5xx for the first few
# attempts and then succeeds, to drive the retry loop deterministically.

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
BENCHMARK="${SCRIPT_DIR}/benchmark"

TMP_ROOT=""
cleanup() {
  if [[ -n "${TMP_ROOT}" && -d "${TMP_ROOT}" ]]; then
    rm -rf "${TMP_ROOT}"
  fi
}
trap cleanup EXIT

fail() {
  echo "FAIL: $*" >&2
  exit 1
}

assert_eq() {
  local got="$1" want="$2" msg="${3:-values differ}"
  [[ "${got}" == "${want}" ]] || fail "${msg}: got=${got} want=${want}"
}

assert_nonempty() {
  local v="$1" msg="${2:-value is empty}"
  [[ -n "${v}" ]] || fail "${msg}"
}

require() {
  command -v "$1" >/dev/null 2>&1 || fail "required tool not found: $1"
}

require jq
require yq
require sha256sum

TMP_ROOT="$(mktemp -d)"
TASKS_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/tasks"
OUT_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/out"
STUB_DIR="${TMP_ROOT}/stubs"
mkdir -p "${TASKS_DIR}" "${OUT_DIR}" "${STUB_DIR}"

PROFILE="sindri-lucebox"
BENCH_SET="tb-2-1-canary"
DATASET="terminal-bench-2-1"
TASKS=(cancel-async-tasks log-summary-date-ranges configure-git-webserver)

echo "==> AC1+AC2: initial sweep produces cells + sweep.json"

env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
    >/dev/null

# sweep.json present with both fields
sweep_json="${OUT_DIR}/sweep.json"
[[ -f "${sweep_json}" ]] || fail "sweep.json missing at ${sweep_json}"
te_ver="$(jq -r '.task_executor_version // ""' "${sweep_json}")"
hd="$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest // ""' "${sweep_json}")"
assert_nonempty "${te_ver}" "sweep.json: task_executor_version empty"
assert_nonempty "${hd}"     "sweep.json: harbor_runner_image_digest empty"
assert_eq "${hd}" "sha256:test-digest-aaa" "sweep.json: digest mismatch"

# One cell per task
for task in "${TASKS[@]}"; do
  task_dir="${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/${task}"
  [[ -d "${task_dir}" ]] || fail "cells dir missing for task=${task}: ${task_dir}"
  cells=("${task_dir}"/*/)
  [[ ${#cells[@]} -eq 1 ]] || fail "task=${task}: expected 1 cell, got ${#cells[@]}"
  cell="${cells[0]%/}"
  report="${cell}/report.json"
  [[ -f "${report}" ]] || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing at ${report}"

  # report.json embedded fields
  [[ "$(jq -r '.profile.id // ""' "${report}")" == "${PROFILE}" ]] \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json profile.id != ${PROFILE}"
  jq -e '.command | type == "array"' "${report}" >/dev/null \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing command array"
  jq -e '.env_redacted | type == "object"' "${report}" >/dev/null \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing env_redacted object"
  jq -e '.task_executor_version' "${report}" >/dev/null \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing task_executor_version"
  jq -e '.harbor_runner_image_digest' "${report}" >/dev/null \
    || fail "task=${task}: report.json missing harbor_runner_image_digest"
  assert_eq "$(jq -r '.task_executor_version' "${report}")" "${te_ver}" \
    "task=${task}: report.json task_executor_version mismatch"
  assert_eq "$(jq -r '.harbor_runner_image_digest' "${report}")" "${hd}" \
    "task=${task}: report.json harbor_runner_image_digest mismatch"

  # Secret redaction: FIZEAU_API_KEY is flagged secret in fiz adapter output
  redacted="$(jq -r '.env_redacted.FIZEAU_API_KEY // ""' "${report}")"
  assert_eq "${redacted}" "***REDACTED***" \
    "task=${task}: FIZEAU_API_KEY should be redacted in env_redacted"

  # Sibling artifacts
  [[ -f "${cell}/fiz.txt" ]] || fail "task=${task}: fiz.txt missing"
  [[ -f "${cell}/fiz.err" ]] || fail "task=${task}: fiz.err missing"
  [[ -d "${cell}/session" ]] || fail "task=${task}: session/ missing"

  # cell-state.json was cleaned up on terminal close
  [[ ! -f "${cell}/cell-state.json" ]] \
    || fail "task=${task}: cell-state.json should be deleted on terminal close"

  # final_status is terminal
  status="$(jq -r '.final_status // ""' "${report}")"
  case "${status}" in
    completed|pass|fail|timeout) ;;
    *) fail "task=${task}: unexpected final_status=${status}" ;;
  esac
done

echo "==> AC3: rerun (no flag) skips terminal cells"

before_count="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells" -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
    >/dev/null
after_count="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells" -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
assert_eq "${after_count}" "${before_count}" "resume default added cells (${before_count} -> ${after_count})"

echo "==> AC4: --retry-invalid reruns invalid cells with attempt_of + superseded_by"

# Mark the cancel-async-tasks cell as invalid by injecting invalid_class.
prior_cell="$(find "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/cancel-async-tasks" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d | head -n1)"
[[ -n "${prior_cell}" ]] || fail "no prior cell found for cancel-async-tasks"
prior_report="${prior_cell}/report.json"
tmp_report="${TMP_ROOT}/prior_report.json"
jq '.invalid_class = "setup_failure" | .final_status = "invalid"' "${prior_report}" >"${tmp_report}"
mv "${tmp_report}" "${prior_report}"
prior_cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' "${prior_report}")"

env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --retry-invalid \
      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
    >/dev/null

# A new cell should now exist whose attempt_of points at the prior cell.
new_cell=""
for c in "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/cancel-async-tasks"/*/; do
  cdir="${c%/}"
  [[ "${cdir}" == "${prior_cell}" ]] && continue
  if [[ -f "${cdir}/report.json" ]]; then
    ao="$(jq -r '.attempt_of // ""' "${cdir}/report.json")"
    if [[ "${ao}" == "${prior_cell}" ]]; then
      new_cell="${cdir}"
      break
    fi
  fi
done
[[ -n "${new_cell}" ]] || fail "retry-invalid: no new cell with attempt_of pointing at ${prior_cell}"

new_cell_id="$(jq -r '.cell_id' "${new_cell}/report.json")"
prior_superseded="$(jq -r '.superseded_by // ""' "${prior_report}")"
assert_eq "${prior_superseded}" "${new_cell_id}" "prior cell missing superseded_by back-link"
echo "    prior=${prior_cell_id} superseded_by=${new_cell_id}"

# Orphan cell-state.json under --retry-invalid: create one, rerun, verify
# new cell links to it via attempt_of.
orphan_dir="${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/log-summary-date-ranges/19700101T000000Z-dead"
mkdir -p "${orphan_dir}"
jq -n --arg p "${PROFILE}" --arg t "log-summary-date-ranges" \
  '{cell_id:"19700101T000000Z-dead", task:$t, profile:$p, started_at:"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", status:"running"}' \
  >"${orphan_dir}/cell-state.json"

env HARBOR_TASK_EXECUTOR_DRY_RUN=1 \
    BENCH_TASKS_DIR="${TASKS_DIR}" \
    BENCH_HARBOR_DIGEST_OVERRIDE="sha256:test-digest-aaa" \
    "${BENCHMARK}" \
      --profile "${PROFILE}" \
      --bench-set "${BENCH_SET}" \
      --reps 1 \
      --retry-invalid \
      --out "${OUT_DIR}" \
    >/dev/null

orphan_report="${orphan_dir}/report.json"
[[ -f "${orphan_report}" ]] || fail "orphan cell did not receive back-written report.json"
orphan_super="$(jq -r '.superseded_by // ""' "${orphan_report}")"
assert_nonempty "${orphan_super}" "orphan cell missing superseded_by"

found_orphan_retry=0
for c in "${OUT_DIR}/cells/${DATASET}/log-summary-date-ranges"/*/; do
  cdir="${c%/}"
  [[ "${cdir}" == "${orphan_dir}" ]] && continue
  if [[ -f "${cdir}/report.json" ]]; then
    ao="$(jq -r '.attempt_of // ""' "${cdir}/report.json")"
    if [[ "${ao}" == "${orphan_dir}" ]]; then
      found_orphan_retry=1
      break
    fi
  fi
done
[[ ${found_orphan_retry} -eq 1 ]] || fail "no retry cell links attempt_of to orphan ${orphan_dir}"

echo "==> AC5: transient 5xx triggers exponential backoff + eventual success"

STATE_FILE="${TMP_ROOT}/transient-state"
echo 0 >"${STATE_FILE}"
cat >"${STUB_DIR}/flaky" <<EOF
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Stub task-executor. Fails the first two attempts with HTTP 503 (a transient
# signature recognized by run_executor_with_retry), then succeeds.
set -euo pipefail
state_file="${STATE_FILE}"
cell_dir=""
spec="\$(cat)"
cell_dir="\$(jq -r '.cell_dir' <<<"\$spec")"
n=\$(cat "\${state_file}")
n=\$((n + 1))
echo "\${n}" >"\${state_file}"
if (( n < 3 )); then
  echo "fake-harbor: HTTP 503 service unavailable" >&2
  exit 1
fi
mkdir -p "\$cell_dir"
jq -n --arg t "\$(jq -r '.task_id' <<<"\$spec")" \\
  '{task_id:\$t, final_status:"completed", status:"completed"}' \\
  >"\$cell_dir/result.json"
EOF
chmod +x "${STUB_DIR}/flaky"

OUT2="${TMP_ROOT}/out2"
mkdir -p "${OUT2}"

test_benchmark_runner.bash
test_preflight_lane.bash
test_runner_loop.bash
test_sweep_shutdown.bash
